pages.title: Project Overview
pages.purpose: High-level introduction to the Private Equity Firm Agentic AI Month-End Close Platform — its business purpose, architecture, the 8 portfolio companies it manages, and how the major subsystems (frontend, backend, agents, workflows) fit together. Links to all child pages for deeper coverage.

pages.title: Getting Started & Configuration
pages.purpose: Step-by-step guide for setting up the development environment: cloning the repo, configuring environment variables (DATABASE_URL, REDIS_URL, GROQ_API_KEY, RESEND_API_KEY, FRONTEND_URL), running the seed script, and starting both the Next.js client and FastAPI server.

pages.title: Dataset & Seed Data
pages.purpose: Explains the financial dataset used by the platform: the 8 portfolio companies (TechForge SaaS, PrecisionMfg Inc, RetailCo, etc.), the CSV/JSON data files in server/data/ (trial_balances/, budgets/, prior_year/, intercompany/, bank_statements/, accrual_schedules/), and how to load them via the seed script (server/seed/load_data.py).

pages.title: Backend Architecture
pages.purpose: Overview of the FastAPI server, its module structure (app/routers, app/agents, app/models, app/workflows, app/tasks, app/sockets, app/email), and how these layers interact. Links to child pages for each major subsystem.

pages.title: Data Models
pages.purpose: Detailed reference for all SQLAlchemy ORM models: Company, TrialBalance, Budget, PriorYear, Alert, IntercompanyTransaction, AgentLog, AgentState, WorkflowRun, and WorkflowHandoff. Covers table schemas, column types, relationships, and how models are initialized via init_db().

pages.title: REST API Routers
pages.purpose: Documents all FastAPI API endpoints grouped by router: /api/companies (list, get, financials), /api/alerts (list, resolve), /api/intercompany, /api/consolidation, /api/variances, /api/workflow (trigger, status, runs, handoffs), and /api/agents (status, logs). Covers request parameters, response shapes, and error handling.

pages.title: Real-Time WebSocket Layer
pages.purpose: Explains the Socket.io ASGI server (server/app/sockets/events.py): how agents emit agent_update events via emit_agent_event(), the connect/disconnect lifecycle, the subscribe_company room mechanism, and how the frontend client (client/src/lib/api.ts getSocket()) connects and listens for live updates.

pages.title: Email Notification System
pages.purpose: Covers the Resend-based email subsystem (server/app/email/sender.py): the send_email_now() function, recipient routing (pe_partners vs cfos), Jinja2 HTML template rendering, and how the Celery beat schedule triggers daily executive summary emails via send_daily_executive_email().

pages.title: Agent System
pages.purpose: Overview of the 10 specialized AI agents built on the Agno framework, how they are constructed (build_* factory functions), the shared base utilities (base.py, tools.py), and how they collaborate in teams. Links to child pages for agent groups and shared infrastructure.

pages.title: Agent Base & Shared Tools
pages.purpose: Deep dive into server/app/agents/base.py and server/app/agents/tools.py: the log_agent_action() and update_agent_state() utilities, the call_llm() / get_groq_model() LLM interface, get_postgres_db(), and all shared tool functions (get_trial_balance, get_budget_for_period, get_accrual_schedule, get_intercompany_transactions, save_alert, log_action, update_company_status, get_consolidation_summary).

pages.title: Orchestrator & Workflow Teams
pages.purpose: Explains the master orchestrator (server/app/agents/orchestrator.py): build_orchestrator_team(), build_per_company_team() (Group 1: Trial Balance Validator + Variance Analysis + Cash Flow Reconciliation), build_sequential_team() (Group 2: Accrual Verification + Revenue Recognition + Expense Categorization), and build_month_end_workflow() using Agno's Workflow/Step/Team primitives. Covers TeamMode.coordinate, the anti-hallucination prompt injection, and the 4-group execution order.

pages.title: Group 1 Agents: Per-Company Validation
pages.purpose: Covers the three agents that run in parallel for each portfolio company in Group 1: Trial Balance Validator (debit/credit balance checks, unexpected balance flags), Variance Analysis Agent (actual vs budget vs prior month, materiality thresholds of 10%/$50K, severity levels, impact labels), and Cash Flow Reconciliation Agent (bank statement matching, outstanding items, indirect method validation).

pages.title: Group 2 Agents: Accounting Verification
pages.purpose: Covers the three sequential agents in Group 2: Accrual Verification Agent (accrual schedule vs trial balance, gap detection, December bonus check, flag_missing_accrual), Revenue Recognition Agent (ASC 606 compliance, performance obligations, timing issues), and Expense Categorization Agent (GL reclassification, cost center validation, chart of accounts improvements).

pages.title: Group 3 & 4 Agents: Cross-Company & Reporting
pages.purpose: Covers the final two agent groups: Group 3 — Intercompany Elimination Agent (fetch_ic_transactions, eliminate_ic_pair, flag_ic_issue, one-sided entry detection across 8 companies); Group 4 — Consolidation Agent (portfolio-wide P&L aggregation, GAAP rules, investor-ready packages) and Reporting & Communication Agent (executive dashboards, email dispatch, board materials).

pages.title: Workflow Engine & Orchestration
pages.purpose: Overview of how the workflow execution layer ties together the agent system, state management, and scheduling. Links to child pages for the engine, state/event bus, and Celery scheduler.

pages.title: MonthEndWorkflowExecutor (Engine)
pages.purpose: Deep dive into server/app/workflows/engine.py: the MonthEndWorkflowExecutor class, the execute() lifecycle, _run_group1_parallel() with ThreadPoolExecutor, the event-driven group transitions (_handle_group1_completed → group2 → group3 → group4), _run_agent_with_retry() with exponential backoff, rate-limit detection, _persist_handoff_db(), and the execute_workflow_run() entry point.

pages.title: Redis State & Event Bus
pages.purpose: Covers RedisWorkflowState (server/app/workflows/state.py): key schema (workflow:{id}, workflow:{id}:handoff:{company}:{stage}), set/get_workflow_meta (hset/hgetall), set/get_handoff (JSON serialization, 7-day TTL). Also covers WorkflowEventBus (event_bus.py): on()/emit() pub-sub pattern and _call_with_retry() for resilient handler execution.

pages.title: Celery Task Scheduler
pages.purpose: Documents server/app/tasks/celery_app.py: the beat_schedule (daily-month-end-close at 9 AM UTC, hourly close-monitor 8 AM–6 PM), the four Celery tasks (run_month_end_close with retry/backoff, monitor_close_progress with fingerprint-based data-drift detection, send_daily_executive_email, agent_health_check stale-runner reaper), and how they integrate with the workflow engine.

pages.title: Frontend Application
pages.purpose: Overview of the Next.js 16 / React 19 client application: its App Router structure, global layout, Tailwind CSS design system, and the pages it exposes. Links to child pages for each major view and the API client layer.

pages.title: Layout, Navigation & Design System
pages.purpose: Covers the global application shell: RootLayout (client/src/app/layout.tsx), the Sidebar component (collapse/expand, optimistic path, navLinks, CFO Access user terminal), the dark-theme design tokens in globals.css (--primary #D4FF3A, --background #18181A, custom-scrollbar, animation keyframes), and Tailwind CSS v4 @theme inline configuration.

pages.title: Dashboard Page
pages.purpose: Documents client/src/app/page.tsx: the Dashboard component, GradientCard stacked-shadow KPI cards (Consol Revenue, EBITDA Margin, Orchestration Agents), portfolio company grid with status bubbles, the handleTrigger workflow button, polling strategy (agentStatus/workflowStatus every 2s, workflowRuns every 5s), workflow state machine label mapping, and the AgentActivityFeed sidebar.

pages.title: Portfolio Companies Views
pages.purpose: Covers two pages: the Companies list page (client/src/app/companies/page.tsx — company cards with traffic-light status indicators) and the Company Detail page (client/src/app/companies/[id]/page.tsx — Variance Analysis tab with AI commentary, Trial Balance tab, Agent Handoffs drawer with Group 1/Group 2 phase tabs, Open Issues panel, ReactMarkdown rendering of agent outputs, MarkdownTableDialog).

pages.title: Reports & Agent Audit Trail Pages
pages.purpose: Covers the Reports page (client/src/app/reports/page.tsx — consolidated financials KPIs, Executive Commentary, intercompany eliminations summary, PDF/Excel/CSV export links, Email Partners button) and the Agents page (client/src/app/agents/page.tsx — Master Trace File table with timestamp, agent name, severity, company, action/reasoning, 5s polling, WebSocket live indicator).

pages.title: Frontend API Client & State Management
pages.purpose: Documents client/src/lib/api.ts: all REST fetch functions (fetchCompanies, fetchCompanyFinancials, fetchConsolidation, fetchAgentStatus, fetchWorkflowStatus, fetchWorkflowRuns, fetchCompanyWorkflowHandoffs, fetchAgentLogs, triggerWorkflow), the Socket.io singleton (getSocket, reconnectionAttempts, transports), and the TanStack React Query / Redux Toolkit dual-layer state architecture.

pages.title: Financial Domain Reference
pages.purpose: Reference guide for the accounting and financial concepts embedded in the platform's business logic: how financial metrics are calculated, the chart of accounts structure, variance materiality rules, consolidation methodology, and the month-end close process flow.

pages.title: Financial Metrics & Calculations
pages.purpose: Precise definitions of all financial metrics computed by the platform: Gross Profit (Revenue − COGS), EBITDA (Gross Profit − OpEx), Net Income (EBITDA − Revenue×5%), EBITDA Margin %, Gross Margin %, close_completion_pct, and how these are aggregated in get_consolidation_summary() and the /api/consolidation endpoint.

pages.title: Variance Analysis & Alert Rules
pages.purpose: Documents the variance analysis logic: materiality thresholds (≥10% or ≥$50K), severity classification (critical: ≥20% or ≥$100K; warning: other material), impact labels (favorable/unfavorable/neutral per account type), prior-period calculation with year-wraparound, the _previous_period helper, and how Alerts are persisted and resolved via the /api/alerts endpoints.

pages.title: Glossary
pages.purpose: Definitions of all codebase-specific terms, financial jargon, abbreviations, and domain concepts that an onboarding engineer needs to understand. Each entry includes code pointers.
